description: Integer-valued JSON token 18446744073709551616 (= 2^64) exceeds the encoder's range [-2^63, 2^64-1]. Writers MUST reject and MUST NOT downcast to Float64.
reject_reason: Integer-valued number above 2^64 - 1; MUST be rejected, not downcast to Float64 (§5.2).

description: Integer-valued JSON token -9223372036854775809 (= -(2^63) - 1) is below the encoder's lower bound -2^63. Writers MUST reject.
reject_reason: Integer-valued number below -2^63; MUST be rejected, not downcast to Float64 (§5.2).

description: Numeric token 1e400 overflows binary64 to +Infinity. JSON has no Infinity literal; writers MUST reject inputs that parse to ±Infinity.
reject_reason: Token parses to Infinity (§5.3): writers MUST NOT produce NaN/Infinity and MUST reject inputs that overflow.

description: Token 1.0000000000000001 needs more significant decimal digits than binary64 represents losslessly: the binary64 parse v differs bit-for-bit from a higher-precision parse v' narrowed to binary64.
reject_reason: Significant decimal digits exceed binary64 precision (§5.3 precision-limit rule).

description: Input JSON object literal contains the key "a" twice. Writers MUST reject across all runs of an object (whether in the same leaf buffer or across already-flushed runs).
reject_reason: Duplicate key in a single object — writers MUST enforce global uniqueness across runs (§11.2).

description: Input contains a string whose bytes are not valid UTF-8 (e.g., a lone continuation byte 0xC0 0xC0 inside a string). The bad bytes cannot be expressed as a valid JSON literal, so json_text is null; raw_input_hex shows the JSON string token's bytes that the writer must reject.
reject_reason: Input string is not valid UTF-8 (§5.4: data MUST be valid UTF-8; §11.2 lists invalid UTF-8 as MUST reject).
